title: Install signal handlers + InFlightTracker + fsync-on-shutdown
description: MCP server has zero signal handlers (index.ts:92-97) — direct hit on Claude Code #15945 zombie pattern. Install SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers. New services/in-flight-tracker.ts tracks active requests; signal triggers drain (5s deadline). Per Gemini G1 (drain-without-fsync paradox): MUST fsyncSync(ledgerFd) AFTER drain BEFORE close. Double-signal triggers hard exit. Test: vitest spawn server via child_process, send real SIGTERM, assert exit code 0 within 5s + ledger fully flushed; Linux/macOS only, Windows skip.
scope: Layer 2 P0 — runtime robustness; closes #15945 zombie pattern + corruption window

files.change: NEW: track in-flight MCP requests; expose drain(deadlineMs) returning Promise<{ drained: number, timed_out: number }>

Install handlers for SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP at startStdioServer (lines 92-97 area). Handler: tracker.drain(5000) → fsyncSync(ledgerFd) → server.close() → process.exit(0). Double-signal hard-exit (process.exit(1) immediately on 2nd signal of same type).
Export getLedgerFd() helper for signal handler to fsync the live fd; alternatively expose drainAndFsync() that the tracker can call

NEW: vitest spawning real server via child_process. it.skipIf(process.platform === 'win32'). Send SIGTERM after 100ms; assert exit code 0 within 5s; assert ledger fully fsynced (open + read after exit)

criteria: Server installs SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP handlers at startup
Drain waits up to 5s for in-flight requests; logs 'drained N requests' to stderr
fsyncSync on ledger fd happens AFTER drain BEFORE server.close (Gemini G1)
vitest spawn test passes on Linux/macOS; skipped on Windows
